text: Welcome to This is My Architecture and today we have Kwanish from Astana International Exchange.
Hello again. So can you tell us about Astana International Exchange?
The mission of our company is build stock markets and provide financial services for Central Asia
region especially for Kazakhstan. Actually we are the first stock exchange who fully operated in the cloud.
Sounds exciting. So what kinds of applications do you have and what kinds of AWS services do you use?
For the core system we are using NASDAQ machine engine. It's located in either EC2 and RDS services.
For anzillar systems we are using ACS, ACR, DynamoDB and different services.
For the high level traffic management we are using Route 53 and for different type of public or private
applications, web applications we are using LB. So that's it. Nice.
Oh I see. So you use RDS for auxiliary services. So running the whole stock exchange in cloud
sounds really exciting. Are there any specific challenges or requirements related to that?
Yes the requirements are very high and especially for high availability. So we built our architecture in
two different regions. It's actually it's traditional approach for any stock exchange but in one different
thing we are operating in cloud and traditionally stock exchange using data centers as classical.
So you are using the second region for even higher availability? Correct.
And how do you replicate your data between regions? For different type of service we're using different
approach. An example for EC2 we're using a life cycle manager that gives us ability to keep actual version of
a snapshot in different region. For RDS we are using Rift Deplica service. It also keeps us the actual version of
database. For the entire version of the database. For EC2 container services we're using ECR.
It gives us the ability to keep the actual version of images in different regions. And for DynamoDB we're using
nothing because we're using global tables that are already presented in different regions.
Oh I see. And what about traffic balancing? How do you balance your network between regions?
Yes for traffic balance as I said before we're using Route 53 and we're using weighted policies. So we can manage
it by sending 100% for active region and 0% for second region. And how do you do a failover?
So we're using the actual version of the server. We've simplified this procedure for one script,
uh shell script. And this script deactivates the actual version here and activates different region.
For RDS database it promotes the second instance in different regions. And for ACR it starts the copy of
images and different regions also. And I'm wondering how long does the failover process take?
Technically it takes 30 seconds but with business approvals it can be taken two or three months.
And do you do any test file overs in your production environment?
Yes, we have certified by ISO 27001. There is a requirement to do this kind of test
minimum twice per year. And also in financial world people said that
stock exchange never starts twice after failover.
Yeah. And what kinds of availability level did you manage to achieve?
Since we built this architecture in the cloud three years ago, there is no
issue or any incident. Awesome. So thank you so much for sharing this information with us. And
thank you for joining This is My Architecture.
